danger_signals.visual_form: UFO/alien craft markers on GeoScape global map
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zone; imminent terror attacks or base assault risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Red alert indicators on seaports and shipping routes in GeoScape
danger_signals.meaning: Civilian population under immediate attack; time-pressured response required

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened, pressure-compromised BattleScape terrain; flooded passages and crushing depths visualization
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger that limits soldier movement and weapon functionality

danger_signals.visual_form: Alien sprites in isometric tactical view; aquatic alien variants distinct from surface enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat requiring tactical response

danger_signals.visual_form: Grayed-out or unavailable weapon loadouts on BattleScape UI; ammunition count reduced compared to surface missions
danger_signals.meaning: Technology limitations due to salt water environment; player must adapt strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Fortified underground/underwater base structure with defensive infrastructure visible in GeoScape
safety_signals.meaning: Protected territory under player control; safe for recruitment, research, and resupply

safety_signals.visual_form: X-COM interceptor/transport markers on GeoScape; distinct from alien craft visual style
Unlocked equipment and weapons visible in inventory/loadout screens
safety_signals.meaning: Evolutionary progression; adaptive capability against aliens

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: X-COM bases marked on GeoScape; underwater installations distinct from surface facilities in first game

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien biological aesthetics; aquatic-adapted creature design distinct from original UFO: Enemy Unknown Sectoids
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cousins of Sectoids; aquatic alien creature sprites with aquatic adaptations visible in BattleScape
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien shipping routes and crash sites underwater; T'Leth spacecraft wreckage in Gulf of Mexico region

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earth's oceans as primary theater; underwater environments replace surface cities from first game
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: X-COM reactivation after disbandment; shift from defensive to aggressive anti-alien strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased alien contact frequency on GeoScape; multiple UFO markers indicate coordinated threat surge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Terror attacks escalate; aliens target seagoing vessels and port cities with organized assault patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Original alien tech rendered inoperable in saltwater; new equipment must be researched and manufactured
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to capture alien technology and reverse-engineer aquatic-compatible systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased alien activity density on GeoScape; final missions converge on Gulf of Mexico region
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coordinated alien attacks intensify; ultimate boss encounter sequence begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multi-part mission structures in BattleScape (e.g., upper/lower ship decks); increased environmental complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Soldiers lost in earlier mission phases do not appear in later phases; cumulative tactical pressure

environmental_storytelling.example: Shift from surface warfare to oceanic environments visually communicates alien threat origin and dormancy revelation
T'Leth spacecraft crash in Gulf of Mexico 65 million years ago; dinosaur extinction connection embedded in world lore and environment design
Multi-type terror missions (shipping route attacks with upper/lower deck structure) demonstrate alien tactical sophistication and environmental knowledge
Non-functional alien weapons and equipment from first game appearing in underwater salvage, then being replaced by new tech—visual representation of technological evolution pressure
X-COM bases reskinned for underwater operation in GeoScape; visual infrastructure distinct from surface predecessor communicates environmental challenge

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Movement constrained by underwater physics; weapon selection limited to saltwater-compatible armaments; speed and endurance affected by pressure/depth
Organized attack patterns on civilian targets; multi-phase mission structure indicates tactical planning; adaptation to underwater combat vs. surface forces
Escalating coordinated attacks; concentration of forces toward Gulf of Mexico; reawakening sequence as narrative climax
Kidnapping for genetic experiments; terror attacks on seaports and shipping routes; helplessness conveyed through mission urgency mechanics

notes: X-COM: Terror from the Deep uses environmental setting (underwater vs. surface) as primary visual and mechanical manifestation of world-state shift. The game communicates threat through location-based danger (alien terror routes on GeoScape), technological degradation (incompatible weapons/equipment), and entity behavioral escalation (coordinated multi-phase attacks). Faction identity relies on creature design variation (aquatic aliens vs. original Sectoids) and environmental infrastructure markers (underwater X-COM bases). Mission complexity and multi-part structure serve as both mechanical and narrative signifiers of escalating alien threat sophistication.
